​✨ ComfyUI Notebook Demo
========================
Points generated : 21
Minimum y value : 0
Maximum y value : 400
Last point      : {'x': 20, 'y': 400}

This demo shows:
- Python code execution
- Data passed between cells
- A generated plot
- A final formatted summary

nodes.title: 1. Build some data
widgets_values:
points = []
for x in range(0, 21):
    points.append({"x": x, "y": x * x})

print("Generated square numbers:")
for p in points:
    print(f"x={p['x']:>2}  y={p['y']:>3}")

Result = points

nodes.title: 2. Plot and summarize
widgets_values:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

xs = [p["x"] for p in input]
ys = [p["y"] for p in input]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 5))
fig.patch.set_facecolor("white")
ax.set_facecolor("#f7f7fb")

ax.plot(xs, ys, linewidth=2.5, marker='o', markersize=7)
ax.fill_between(xs, ys, alpha=0.15)

ax.set_title("Square Numbers", fontsize=16, pad=12, weight='bold')
ax.set_xlabel("x", fontsize=12)
ax.set_ylabel("x²", fontsize=12)
ax.grid(True, linestyle='--', alpha=0.35)

for spine in ["top", "right"]:
    ax.spines[spine].set_visible(False)

ax.annotate(
    f"({xs[-1]}, {ys[-1]})",
    xy=(xs[-1], ys[-1]),
    xytext=(xs[-1] - 2.2, ys[-1] - 18),
    arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', lw=1.5),
    fontsize=10
)

ax.margins(x=0.05, y=0.1)
plt.tight_layout()

summary = {
    "count": len(input),
    "max_y": max(ys),
    "min_y": min(ys),
    "last_point": input[-1]
}

print("Created nicer plot and summary")
Result = summary
